items.summary: The US president again attacks America's allies over their reluctance to get involved in the Iran war, warning them the "US won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

items.title: King Charles confirms US state visit amid growing European tensions over Iran war
items.summary: US President Donald Trump lashed out at the UK just before the visit was confirmed as France, Spain and Italy restrict airspace to US war planes.

items.title: House prices rise for first quarter of 2026, but 'widening divergence' between capital cities paints future picture
items.summary: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

items.title: WA's greyhound racing regulator defends track record at inquiry into sport's future
items.summary: The body governing greyhound racing in WA says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both the dogs and the industry at a parliamentary inquiry, which was sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

items.title: Family of Zomi Frankcom still waiting for answers two years after death at hands of IDF
items.summary: Two years ago Australian aid worker Zomi Frankcom was killed by an IDF strike in Gaza. Her family is still waiting for answers and accountability.

items.title: Socceroos thump Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell, as Irankunda electrifies
items.summary: Nestory Irankunda scores twice as the Socceroos' substitutes spark a 5-1 win over Curaçao in their FIFA Series clash in Melbourne.

items.title: Dezi Freeman's nephew Luke Filby speaks out about his 'evil' uncle's death at hands of Victoria police
items.summary: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead, it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Daniel Ziffer
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Thousands of Chinese social media users change profile photos to Kris Jenner
items.summary: Thousands of Chinese social media users have started using images of US celebrity Kris Jenner as their profile photos in hopes it will bring them success and good fortune.

items.title: Barbie fans paid hundreds of dollars for a festival in Florida. Here's why they weren't impressed
items.summary: Barbie fans who paid up to $656 for an "unforgettable experience" were met with a concrete-floored warehouse and flimsy pink cardboard cut-outs.

items.title: Israel's ambassador defends new death penalty for Palestinians

items.title: Inquest into Mackay grandmother's death hears of 'shortcomings' in private hospital's after-hours staffing
items.summary: A coroner finds the post-surgery care of Robyn Beale at Mater Private Hospital Mackay was not "adequate or appropriate", but the inquest heard the 78-year-old would have died regardless.

items.title: Devastated artists rally after Daly River floods destroy Merrepen Arts Centre

items.summary: As authorities commit to a review of problems faced by South Australian voters during this month's election, a former Liberal MP says he was concerned to received an extra ballot paper in the post.

items.summary: Huge amounts of false images and video circulating about the conflict in Iran are often making it difficult to distinguish genuine news from manipulated fakery, but this one is real.

items.title: Chicken and eggs could end up cheaper because of new high-protein sorghum variety

items.title: Consumer confidence hits 53-year low, ASX gains — as it happened

Catch up on the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi half-century leads Rajasthan Royals to IPL win over Chennai Super Kings

items.title: Amid fuel price rises, business owners plead for kindness as abuse from customers increases

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent
items.summary: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

items.title: 50 per cent of Australians avoid health care they need due to cost, according to Consumers Health Forum report
items.summary: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

items.title: Early government modelling assumes fuel rationing when stockpile falls to 10 days' diesel supply

items.title: Fuel purchasing powers could be used for nearly anything facing supply disruption

items.title: Insiders profit as Iran war pressure points build on global economy
items.summary: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

items.title: If your home is underinsured it comes with big risks, experts say
items.summary: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

items.title: Bittersweet Iraqi film The President's Cake brings hope as war rages in the Middle East
items.summary: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

items.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iranian oil and energy sites if it refuses deal to end war
items.summary: As thousands more US troops arrive in the Middle East, Donald Trump threatens to wipe out Iran's energy plants and oil wells if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: China keeps Iranian oil moving through Hormuz as war reshapes trade
items.summary: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

items.title: Myanmar's junta chief Min Aung Hlaing steps down to pursue presidency
items.summary: Myanmar's military chief has stepped down to seek the presidency in the general's latest efforts to maintain power behind the facade of a civilian administration.

items.title: Five social media platforms investigated over compliance with under-16 ban
items.summary: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.
